commentaries.author: Aquinas Study Bible
commentaries.content: The apostle Jude, called Thaddaeus, whom Matthew 10:3 and Mark 3:18 mentioned in their Gospels. (St. Bede) He was the brother of James the Less. (Bishop Richard Challoner)

commentaries.author: Clement Of Alexandria
commentaries.content: Jude, a servant of Jesus Christ,— of Him as Lord; but the brother of James. For this is true; he was His brother, (the son) of Joseph.

commentaries.author: Clement Of Alexandria
commentaries.content: Jude, who wrote the Catholic Epistle, the brother of the sons of Joseph, and very religious, while knowing the near relationship of the Lord, yet did not say that he himself was His brother.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
And brother of James, the apostle and bishop of Jerusalem; he might have added, the brother of Christ, as he and the same St. James are so styled; i.e. cousin germans.
And called. That is, to all converted to the faith of Christ, whether they were Jews or Gentiles. (Witham)
